items.question: If A is 100×5 with rank 5, how many non-zero singular values does it have (up to numerical tolerance)?
items.explanation: Rank r ≤ min(m,n); at most r singular values are strictly positive (here r ≤ 5).

items.question: Which matrix has eigenvalues equal to squared singular values of A?
items.explanation: σᵢ² are eigenvalues of the symmetric matrix AᵀA.

items.question: Truncated SVD with rank k minimizes which norm of A - A_k?
items.explanation: Eckart–Young: best rank-k approximation in Frobenius and spectral norms.

items.question: For centered data matrix X, PCA principal components correspond to:
options: Rows of U from SVD of X
Columns of V from SVD of X
items.answer: Columns of V from SVD of X
items.explanation: Right singular vectors (rows of Vᵀ) are principal directions of centered X.
